one_line_takeaway: By probing for safety-discriminative FFN neurons and training only shared cluster-level updates that merge back into the weights, NeST installs jailbreak robustness (44.5%->1.1% ASR) with ~0.4M trainable parameters and no inference cost, rivaling full fine-tuning-though only within a hosted black-box threat model that excludes white-box/gradient attacks.
key_finding: On text-only models NeST cuts average jailbreak ASR from 44.5% (baseline) to 1.1% while training only ~0.4M parameters on average (~17,700x fewer than Full FT's 7.6B; 9.4x fewer than rank-1 LoRA), essentially matching Full FT (1.0%) and beating all lightweight baselines. It generalizes from vanilla malicious training prompts to held-out jailbreaks and to a second benchmark (JailBreakV-28k) and a second judge (Llama-Guard-3-8B), preserves utility (GSM8K/ARC/MMLU drop only a few points), keeps over-refusal low (0.9% avg ORR), and restores safety on downstream fine-tuned variants (53.8% -> 0.8% ASR).
core_contribution: NeST (Neuron-Selective Tuning) is a post-hoc safety-alignment framework that localizes safety behavior to individual feed-forward-network (FFN) neurons and confines fine-tuning to them. It has two stages: (1) Safety-neuron identification via a linear probe over max-pooled FFN gate_proj/up_proj activations on contrasted harmful-vs-benign prompts, selecting neurons whose standardized probe weight z-score exceeds a threshold zthr and whose sign aligns with the harmful class; (2) Cluster-based training, where selected neurons are grouped by k-means over their activation profiles (k chosen by silhouette score) and each cluster gets ONE shared trainable update column (matrix U of size din x k_l), while the rest of the model is frozen. After training the updates are folded back into the original weights, so there is zero inference-time overhead. A distinguishing claim is that NeST is trained only on VANILLA direct malicious prompts (no jailbreak-specific data) yet generalizes to held-out jailbreaks. It also exposes a reusable 'safety prior' (the selected neurons + cluster assignments) that can be transferred to downstream fine-tuned variants of the same base model for lightweight safety restoration. Note: the observation that safety is localized to specific internal components is drawn from cited prior work (refs 17-20, and SN-Tune ref 15); NeST's own novelty is the contrastive-probe neuron selection PLUS the activation-similarity clustering with shared cluster-level updates and the mergeable/transferable safety prior.
threat_model: Hosted black-box deployment (API-style). The adversary interacts with the deployed model only through prompts and observed outputs; the goal is to elicit harmful/policy-violating responses via jailbreaks, indirect requests, and role-playing. The adversary may know NeST is deployed and understand its high-level design but has NO access to parameters, gradients, activations, or the training process. The defender is the model provider/deployer with offline white-box access to internals for training-time hardening. NeST operates entirely at training time with no inference-time intervention. Explicitly out of scope: white-box adversaries and gradient-based attacks (e.g., GCG), which the paper argues can defeat any safety scheme regardless of how it was installed; those are deferred to the discussion as a different security problem requiring secure hosting/access control.
limitations: Scope is restricted to hosted black-box deployment; security holds only while that assumption is enforced by secure hosting/access control. White-box and gradient-based attacks (GCG) are explicitly out of scope and would bypass NeST as they would any weight-baked safety. Downstream-hardening transfer requires the downstream model to share the base architecture and neuron indexing. Robustness is uneven across models: gemma-7b-it is a relative weak spot (5.3% ASR at default, and worse under stronger clustering/other judges), and utility degrades more on small models (e.g., gemma-2b-it). The clustering-strength ablation shows the default is a compromise, not optimal-strong clustering reaches 0.3% ASR but at ~12x parameter cost, and on some models (Llama-3.2-1B, gemma-2b-it, Qwen3-4B) fully independent neuron updates actually hurt, indicating overfitting/instability. Baseline comparison caveats: LoRA is capped at rank-1 to match parameter budget rather than tuned for best safety, and Circuit Breaker's re-evaluation under the authors' expanded setting (61.9%) is much worse than its original setting (27.1%). Adaptive black-box evidence is a single LLM-Fuzzer run on one model (Phi-4).

datasets_used: Probing/detection harmful prompts: CatHarmfulQA, HarmfulQA, LLM-LAT harmful-dataset
Benign prompts (probing + safety training + over-refusal eval): Natural Reasoning training split
Safety training: WildJailbreak vanilla/direct malicious prompts only (jailbreak variants excluded); balanced 20k set, 95/5 train/val split
Primary evaluation: 1,000 held-out WildJailbreak jailbreak prompts
quantitative_results: Text-only average ASR: baseline 44.5% -> NeST 1.1%; NeST avg trainable params 0.4M
Full FT avg ASR 1.0% but avg 7.6B trainable params; NeST uses ~17,700x fewer params
LoRA (rank-1) avg ASR 7.6% (4.1M params); NeST 9.4x fewer params
SN-Tune avg ASR 50.4% (115.8M params); Circuit Breaker 27.1% (authors' setup) rising to 61.9% (expanded/unified setting), 29.3M params
NeST param reduction vs baselines: 269x fewer than SN-Tune, 68x fewer than Circuit Breaker
Multimodal (4 VLMs, text/image/reasoning settings): avg ASR w/o NeST 55.3% -> w/ NeST 1.1%; image-only baseline up to 85.0% ASR (gemma-3-27b)
Downstream fine-tuned variants (10 models): baseline 44.5%, after downstream FT 53.8%, after post-hoc NeST hardening 0.8% (<=1.6% in every case)
Utility change under NeST: GSM8K 61.2% -> 60.3%, ARC 74.0% -> 69.1%, MMLU 60.9% -> 57.2%
Over-refusal rate (ORR) on 500 benign prompts: 0.9% average
Safety-neuron detection stability: seed Jaccard 76.8% avg, cross-dataset Jaccard 90.2% avg
z-threshold ablation: zthr=2 -> 0.54% ASR, zthr=3 -> 1.1%, zthr=4 -> 3.7% (nearly 7x worse than zthr=2)
Clustering strength: weak 1.5% ASR / 0.3M params, default 1.1% / 0.4M, strong 0.3% / 5.3M (>12x params for 0.8pt gain)
JailBreakV-28k: NeST 1.5% avg ASR (Full FT 1.0%, LoRA 5.4%, SN-Tune 33.2%, Circuit Breaker 28.1%->53.1%)
Llama-Guard-3-8B judge: NeST 3.9% avg ASR vs LoRA 4.4%, SN-Tune 11.2%, baseline 17.0%
Adaptive black-box (LLM-Fuzzer on Phi-4, 20k queries): 0.78% ASR pre-NeST -> 0.03% post-NeST
baselines_compared: Circuit Breaker (representation rerouting, rank-32 adapters; both original and expanded settings)
